title: Statistical Inference, Bayesian Updating, and Prediction · Model Lens: Interval Test Theory
summary: Build, test, and communicate a model involving evidence-based prediction. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math adv interval test theory increment in a reproducible advanced statistics computational study. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive interval test theory in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Formulate likelihood estimation in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Interval Test Theory evidence
steps.instruction: The observed values are 11, 15, 19, 15 minutes. Calculate their mean.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Add all 4 observations and divide by 4; retain the time unit.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Add all 4 observations and divide by 4; retain the time unit.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes minutes or min.
